title: Crispy Asian Pork Stir-fry
description: A rich and comforting Polish dish featuring crispy pork slices in a blend of pineapple juice and other aromatic seasonings, perfect for a light dinner that's both low-fat-friendly and satisfying.

ingredients.name: pork chop marinade
ingredients.notes: including soy sauce, rice vinegar, and sesame oil

ingredients.name: cornstarch
ingredients.notes: for dredging pork chops in a light batter

instructions: Mix pork chop marinade in a bowl. Season the pork loin with salt and coat each piece lightly with cornstarch.
Heat cooking spray or olive oil over medium-high heat in a wok or large skillet. Add pineapple chunks, garlic, and sugar, sautéing until caramelized (about 5 minutes).
Add marinated pork to the skillet. Let cook for about 2-3 minutes on each side until golden brown.
Deglaze the pan with pork stock and pour in a cup of pineapple juice. Stir well to combine all ingredients, adding more pineapple juice if necessary for desired consistency.
Bring to a boil, reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 5-7 minutes or until sauce thickens slightly.
Add chopped green onions just before serving. Garnish with additional green onions if desired.
